title: Container Packaging, Startup, Health, Migration Coordination, and Rollout · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds deployable emitted TypeScript feed service from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of deployable emitted TypeScript feed service while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Inspect packed ESM exports, emitted JavaScript, maps, production dependencies, migrations, licenses, and startup command in a clean Node 24 environment.
Build a pinned non-root minimal image with exact package, certificates, ownership, metadata, SBOM, signature, and vulnerability evidence.
Validate configuration, dependency compatibility, migration state, secrets, budgets, and separate startup, liveness, readiness, degradation, and maintenance semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Package Artifact
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 6479 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bind package, non-root container, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Package Artifact.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect packed esm exports, emitted javascript, maps, production dependencies, migrations, licenses, and startup command in a clean node 24 environment. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Package Artifact, keep Feed Ts Package Artifact connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
